item.title: Blockbuster Season Is Back — and the Theater Still Matters
item.body:
## The heart of the movie calendar

Late June sits at the center of Hollywood's most important stretch of the year. The summer box office has long been where studios place their biggest bets — the sequels, the spectacles, and the crowd-pleasers built for the biggest screens they can find. For the industry, these weeks aren't just entertainment; they're the financial engine that funds much of what comes later.

## Why the big screen holds on

Streaming has changed how most of us watch most things, yet the theatrical event has proven surprisingly durable. There's a reason for that.

- **Scale you can't replicate at home.** Certain films — the space epics, the action tentpoles, the animated adventures — are engineered for a room the size of a warehouse and a sound system to match.
- **The shared reaction.** A packed house gasping, laughing, and applauding together turns a movie into an event. That collective energy simply doesn't travel through a laptop.
- **The cultural clock.** Big summer releases still set the conversation. Being there opening weekend means being part of the moment before the internet spoils it.

## What to watch for this season

You don't need a critic's eye to read the summer slate. A few patterns tell the story:

1. **The franchise anchors.** Established series draw built-in audiences and dominate the opening weekends.
2. **The original surprise.** Nearly every summer, a non-sequel breaks out on word of mouth and outperforms expectations. Those are often the most fun to catch early.
3. **The family window.** With school out, animated and all-ages films find long, steady runs rather than a single explosive weekend.

## Make it an event

If the summer blockbuster is going to survive, it does so because audiences choose to show up. So pick the film that promises the most spectacle, gather a few friends, and go for the full experience — the oversized screen, the popcorn, the crowd. Some movies are meant to be watched at home. The ones built for this season are meant to be watched *together*, in the dark, with strangers cheering at the same moment you are.
